授業目的: This course introduces Japanese art and culture from the medieval through the twentieth century. Taking a historical and interdisciplinary approach, the course aims to achieve an in-depth understanding of Japanese culture. Topics covered in the course include peripheral cultures, foreigners in Edo, religious beliefs and practices, and folk figures. This course also offers students opportunities to learn from first-hand experience through fieldwork.
到達目標: In this course students will gain an in-depth understanding of Japanese culture and will be able to identify some of representative Japanese artworks. They will also become able to discuss and analyze several aspects of Japanese culture as well as related concepts and theories.
